get.summary: Retrieves the details of a specific EC order by its unique order ID.
get.description: Note: The StartDate, EndDate, and UpdateDate properties in the response represent calendar dates without time zone. They should not be interpreted as UTC or local time, and must not be converted between time zones. The OrderDateUtc property is a UTC datetime, converted from local time, and includes time information. 409 Conflict: This status is returned if the order is found, but required associated data (such as decision or person) is missing.

parameters.name: orderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier (GUID) of the order.

get.summary: Retrieves the content of a specific attached PDF document by its document ID.

parameters.name: documentId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the PDF document.

get.summary: Gets the count of not accepted EC orders for a specified unit.

parameters.name: unitId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the unit.

get.summary: Retrieves all EC orders for a specified unit, optionally filtered by person.
get.description: Note: The StartDate, EndDate, and UpdateDate properties in the response represent calendar dates without time zone. They should not be interpreted as UTC or local time, and must not be converted between time zones. The OrderDateUtc property is a UTC datetime, converted from local time, and includes time information.

parameters.name: unitId
parameters.description: The unique identifier of the unit. (Required)

parameters.name: personId
parameters.description: The identifier of the person to filter orders by. (Optional)

get.summary: Lists metadata for all PDF attachments associated with a specific external order.

parameters.name: externalOrderId
parameters.description: The unique identifier (GUID) of the external order.

get.summary: Checks the Journal Digital batchjob status.

post.summary: Post an attatchment to be bound to an actualisation

Starts a background batch process to handle Journal Digital entries for the specified domain and key,
beginning from the given date. The key should be sent as an X-API-Key in the header for better security.
post.description:
This endpoint is typically used to initiate background processing jobs for Journal Digital entries.
Ensure that the provided domain and key in the request object are valid for the intended operation.
Sending the key in the querystring is supported but not recommended due to security concerns.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description:
The request body containing additional parameters for the batch process, such as the fromDate, the date from which to start processing digital journal entries.
            Only entries on or after this date are included. If this value is omitted, the last successful integration date stored in the customer data will be used.

OrderECDetailsDTO.description: Detailed information about an EC order.

RegistrationProtection.description: Indicates whether the person has protected registration (skyddad folkbokföring).

AddressProtection.description: Indicates whether the person has address protection (adresskydd eller sekretessmarkering).

StartDate.description: The start date of the order period. Note: This is a calendar date without time zone. Do not interpret as UTC or local time. Do not convert between time zones; use the value directly as a date.

EndDate.description: The end date of the order period. Note: This is a calendar date without time zone. Do not interpret as UTC or local time. Do not convert between time zones; use the value directly as a date.

OrderDateUtc.description: The date and time the order was created, in UTC. Note: This value is converted from local time to UTC and includes time information.

UpdateDate.description: The date the order was last updated. Note: This is a calendar date without time zone. Do not interpret as UTC or local time. Do not convert between time zones; use the value directly as a date.
